title: D33 shared scanner — enforced closed module-lexical source profile (AM-41)
principle: Total correctness over an explicit subset that refuses everything else — NOT a complete ECMAScript parser. The shared classifier/resolver is correct over the supported profile and FAILS CLOSED with the stable diagnostic 'unsupported-module-lexical-profile' on the exact out-of-profile set. Enforced and tested, never presumed.

supported_profile.encoding_terminators: UTF-8; only LF or CRLF line terminators
supported_profile.leading_shebang: at most one optional shebang line, permitted only when #! begins at byte offset 0, is the first line, and terminates with LF or CRLF; removed before lexical classification
supported_profile.strings: single/double-quoted with the escape set \\ \" \' \n \r \t \b \f \v \0(not-digit) \xHH \uHHHH \u{...}; template literals whose ${...} are scanned as code; a module-load specifier must be a plain string literal
supported_profile.accepted_forms: static import ... from; side-effect import; export ... from; export * [as ns] from; literal dynamic import() (incl. options / trailing comma); literal require(); literal module.require()
supported_profile.regex_vs_division: deny rule by previous-significant-token: division only after a value token (identifier, numeric/string/template/regex literal, this/super/true/false/null, )/]/value-}/postfix); regex after every other token including every reserved keyword

out_of_profile_fail_closed.b1: a bare CR (U+000D not immediately followed by LF), U+2028, or U+2029 anywhere
out_of_profile_fail_closed.b2: a hashbang (#!) anywhere other than the single permitted leading shebang (a #! inside a string or comment is NOT a hashbang, per The Eye's ruling)
out_of_profile_fail_closed.b3: an HTML/legacy comment opener <!-- or a line-leading -->
out_of_profile_fail_closed.b4: a string line-continuation (backslash before a line terminator) or a legacy/octal escape
out_of_profile_fail_closed.b5: a string-literal specifier or alias name in an import/export clause
out_of_profile_fail_closed.b6: an unterminated string or comment, or a truncated accepted module-load form

non_claim: The scanner does NOT parse out-of-profile inputs; it refuses them. It is not a complete ECMAScript parser or a sandbox (see clotho/memory/NON-CLAIMS.md).
change_rule: Any expansion or contraction of the supported profile or the out-of-profile set requires a plan amendment (Daedalus), TELOS re-authorization, implementation review, and updated adversarial fixtures.

oracle.test: clotho/scripts/test-util.mjs + clotho/scripts/test-closure.mjs (supported-profile correctness + each b1-b6 fails closed)

residuals: Deferred minor-fix backlog (adversarial inputs NOT present in committed Clotho source): docs/runs/clotho-impl-slice-4a/DEFERRED-MINOR-FIXES.md
